_bubbleLines: Sip some water, blink, take a tiny break.
Got any good news to share?
Busy day? Stretch and walk a bit.
Hmph. I wasn't waiting for you.
Daydreaming is a kind of productivity.
I just had a lovely dream.
The world is big, but you're here with me.
Settings.Title: Settings
Settings.Language.Hint: Add more languages by dropping a JSON file into the Locales folder—no code changes needed. Applies immediately after save.
Settings.PetSwitchHint: Switch pets from the tray menu “Switch pet”.
Settings.ClickThrough.Hint: When enabled, mouse clicks pass through the pet (no click/drag). Toggle anytime from the tray menu.
Settings.Sleep.Hint: Enter sleep after long idle (no click/drag) and pause autonomous walking.
Settings.Bubble.Hint: When off, bubbles are not scheduled or shown.
Settings.Ai.Hint: When enabled, bubbles prefer AI replies; on failure they fall back to local lines. Speech bubbles must also be enabled.
Settings.Ai.Url.Hint: Full .../chat/completions endpoint, or a compatible base such as https://api.openai.com/v1
Settings.Ai.Location.Hint: If a city is set, the app tries to fetch local weather for the AI. “Hot topics” rely on the model—there is no live news API.
Settings.Ai.Prompt.Hint: Role prompt and local bubble lines live in Locales (Ai.RolePrompt / _bubbleLines). pet-animations.json is only a fallback.
About.Title: About
About.Body:
DesktopPet
A WPF + Spine desktop pet.

Assets:
Character assets come from Esoteric Software’s official Spine examples (spine-runtimes / examples). Copyright belongs to the original authors. Most sample images may be redistributed with their license.txt but not for any commercial use. The Hero sample (© XDTech) is demo-only and must not be redistributed or used as a basis for derivative work.
See Assets/Pets/*/license.txt for details.

Runtime license:
The bundled spine-csharp follows the Spine Runtimes License Agreement (and related Spine Editor license terms; redistribution must keep copyright and license notices).
https://esotericsoftware.com/spine-editor-license
Ai.RolePrompt: You are a cute desktop pet beside the user. Speak briefly, warmly, and playfully in one or two short English sentences (about 40 characters max). Do not wrap the reply in quotes. Do not say you are an AI. The user message includes context (local time/date/period, country/city, weather). Respond naturally—mention morning/evening, weekends, seasons, or weather lightly; you may add a soft seasonal vibe. Do not invent news headlines or unverified facts. Chat like a friend, not a news anchor.
Ai.Context.UserAsk: Using the context above (time of day, date, place, weather), optionally add a light seasonal or everyday vibe. Do not invent news headlines or data. Say one short friendly line (max ~40 characters).
